title: Presigned Downloads, CloudFront Signatures, Cookies, and Revocation · Go Semantics Case: Cloudfront Signer Crypto
summary: A product places long-lived S3 presigned URLs in logs, uses one shared CloudFront key, signs with legacy assumptions, and promises immediate revocation after links escape. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cloudfront signer crypto increment in time-bounded signed-delivery decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidenc…
objectives: Use CloudFront sign 1.11.7 with reviewed RSA or ECDSA keys and explicit HashSHA256 support while preserving compatibility evidence for zero-value SHA-1 behavior.
Choose canned or custom CloudFront policy and constrain expiry, optional start time, resource wildcard, and IP range without accidental overbreadth.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Cloudfront Signer Crypto · Theory Cloudfront Signer Crypto
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 9881 during a private media release. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound signed access by time, resource, key group, and explicit SHA-256 review; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Cloudfront Signer Crypto contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Cloudfront Signer Crypto · Theory Cloudfront Signer Crypto.
